content:
## Intake Classification
Use this workflow when the customer reports a debit-card transaction they did not authorize and the card was not presented at the merchant. Treat the report under the liability categories unauthorized_transaction, card_present_fraud, card_not_present_fraud. If the facts point to a non-unauthorized error instead, the customer liability amount is 0 and this card-not-present action path does not control.

## Account Protection
For a confirmed card-not-present fraud intake, set the card action to close_and_reissue. Do not leave the existing card usable after accepting this category. If the customer is still gathering details, collect the missing transaction information before taking the action; if the category changes, follow the document for that category instead.

## Liability Timing
Apply the liability cap based on when the customer reported the issue after discovery:
- Reported within 2 business days: maximum liability 50.
- Reported after that period but still within the statement window: maximum liability 500.
- Reported after the statement window: unlimited liability in this benchmark policy, represented by -1.

## Provisional Credit
Card-not-present fraud is eligible for provisional credit because eligible categories are unauthorized_transaction, card_present_fraud, card_not_present_fraud, atm_cash_discrepancy, duplicate_charge. See "Debit Card Provisional Credit Policy" for credit handling and "Internal: Debit Card Dispute Intake Requirements" for intake evidence requirements.
title: Internal: Card Not Present Fraud Handling
